answers.answer_rationale: The full text focuses on NLP for process extraction from NL requirements and does not discuss process redesign, matching, prediction, sentiment analysis, etc. as the primary scope.
The paper explicates a concrete method (Visual Narrator) and describes its extraction algorithm.
supporting_quotes: We present the Visual Narrator: a fully automated, open-source tool that implements our algorithm and generates conceptual models as OWL ontologies.
Visual Narrator implements the DERIVECM procedure and uses a Miner/PatternIdentiﬁer/PatternFactory pipeline to extract models from user stories.

answers.answer_rationale: The full text reports evaluation with two case studies and metrics of recall/precision.
supporting_quotes: We evaluate the feasibility and usefulness of our approach by discussing the output of Visual Narrator with leading analysts from the companies that supplied the two data sets.
Our evaluation on two case studies showed promising accuracy results

candidate_title: Extracting business process entities and relations from text using pre-trained language models and in-context learning

source_record_provenance.record_title: Extracting business process entities and relations from text using pre-trained language models and in-context learning

answers.answer_rationale: Full text of the paper is provided and discusses the topic in English; the abstract clearly presents the research on process extraction from text using NLP.
supporting_quotes: Extracting Business Process Entities and Relations from Text Using Pre-trained Language Models and In-Context Learning
Abstract. The extraction of business processes elements from textual documents is a research area which still lacks the ability to scale to the variety of real-world texts.

answers.answer_rationale: The paper presents an original investigation into using GPT-3 with in-context learning for process extraction, describing a novel approach and empirical assessment.
supporting_quotes: In this paper we explore, for the first time in literature, the feasibility of using in-context learning over the pre-trained language models to perform process extraction from textual documents.
The experiments we have conducted provide two types of insights.

answers.answer_rationale: The paper explicitly targets process extraction from text using NLP; defines entities (activities, participants) and relations and discusses an NLP-based extraction pipeline.
supporting_quotes: The extraction of activities, participants, the performing relation between a participant and the activity(ies) it performs, and the sequence relation between activities
In-Context Learning to Process Extraction from Text
we decided to start from GPT-3 ... and explore the feasibility of using it in its native form as well as with two customizations

answers.answer_rationale: The paper focuses on NLP-driven process extraction from natural language text; there is no discussion of process redesign, matching, prediction, sentiment analysis, or generating natural text from processes.
The paper explicates a concrete method: prompts for GPT-3 with in-context learning, a pipeline to extract activities, participants, relations, and precedence, plus an empirical assessment.
